la-belle-noiseuse.reason: Four-hour Rivette meditation on art and obsession that earns every minute.
la-belle-noiseuse.consensus: 100% RT - Cannes winner, art house essential

utamaro-and-his-five-women.reason: If it's screening at an art house with 100% RT, it's Mizoguchi being Mizoguchi.

hamlet.reason: Shakespeare on the big screen is always worth it, especially with perfect RT score suggesting solid adaptation.

louie-bluie.reason: Terry Zwigoff documenting Howard Armstrong - if you know, you know.

the-shop-around-the-corner.reason: Lubitsch's romantic perfection - the template for every enemies-to-lovers story ever told.

no-other-choice.reason: 97% RT for a theatrical release suggests this documentary or drama earned its stripes.

three-colors-blue.reason: Kieslowski's trilogy opener with Juliette Binoche processing grief through color and sound - transcendent.

tt0042593.reason: Nicholas Ray's masterpiece with Bogart at his most vulnerable - essential noir that digs into toxic masculinity.

wall-e.reason: Pixar's robot romance works as both kids' entertainment and environmental wake-up call.
wall-e.consensus: Pixar masterpiece combining stunning animation with meaningful themes.

crumb.reason: Terry Zwigoff's portrait of underground cartoonist Robert Crumb is uncomfortable in all the right ways.
crumb.consensus: Acclaimed documentary offering unflinching look at controversial artist.

monty-python-and-the-holy-grail.reason: The Python troupe's medieval absurdist comedy plays even better with a crowd.
monty-python-and-the-holy-grail.consensus: Comedy classic beloved by audiences and critics.

steal-this-story-please-2026.reason: Documentary about Amy Goodman from the team behind The Janes should be essential viewing about press freedom.
steal-this-story-please-2026.consensus: Documentary about journalist Amy Goodman from Oscar-nominated filmmakers.

fantastic-planet.reason: René Laloux's trippy animated sci-fi from 1973 is unlike anything else you'll see.
fantastic-planet.consensus: Cult animated classic with unique visual style.

suzhou-river.reason: Chinese sixth generation cinema can be rewarding but requires patience for its contemplative pace.
suzhou-river.consensus: Chinese art film with strong critical reception.

three-colors-white.reason: Kieślowski's middle film in his trilogy follows a Polish man's elaborate revenge after his French wife divorces him. Essential European cinema that balances dark comedy with genuine pathos.

rocco-and-his-brothers.reason: Visconti's epic about a southern Italian family moving to Milan is a masterclass in neorealism. Long but rewarding for serious film buffs.

camera-buff.reason: Kieślowski's early film about amateur filmmaking becoming obsession is meta cinema gold. Film lovers will find it essential.

barton-fink.reason: Coen Brothers' surreal Hollywood nightmare starring John Turturro as a blocked playwright. Peak weird Coens for cinephiles.

nostalghia.reason: Tarkovsky's meditative poem about exile and memory is slow cinema at its most spiritual. Not for everyone but transcendent for the patient.

let-the-sunshine-in.reason: Denis follows Juliette Binoche through messy romantic entanglements in middle age. French relationship drama for a specific mood.

john-wick.reason: Keanu's dog-revenge actioner revitalized the genre with clean choreography and world-building. Peak modern action filmmaking.

peppermint-candy.reason: Lee Chang-dong's backwards-told tragedy through Korean history is devastating and essential. Serious Korean cinema at its finest.

inland-empire.reason: Lynch's three-hour digital nightmare about an actress trapped in recursive Hollywood hell is his most challenging work. For Lynch completists and those who like their movies incomprehensible.

kika.reason: No plot details available but it's likely European arthouse. Title alone doesn't give much to work with.

art-school-confidential.reason: Zwigoff's follow-up to Ghost World landed with a thud and 35% shows why.

invention-for-destruction.reason: Zeman's animation masterpiece is a visual marvel that influenced generations of filmmakers.

the-player.reason: Altman's Hollywood satire is sharp as ever and Tim Robbins is perfectly slimy as a studio exec who gets away with murder.
the-player.consensus: Widely praised as one of the best films about Hollywood.

chronicle-of-the-years-of-fire.reason: Lakhdar-Hamina's Palme d'Or winner about Algerian independence is a rare chance to see essential world cinema.
chronicle-of-the-years-of-fire.consensus: Acclaimed historical epic, Palme d'Or winner.

goodbye-dragon-inn.reason: Tsai Ming-liang's meditation on cinema's death is pure poetry for film lovers.
goodbye-dragon-inn.consensus: Acclaimed arthouse film about movie theaters closing.

the-headless-woman.reason: Martel's enigmatic thriller about a woman who may have killed someone is haunting and brilliant.

vive-l-amour.reason: Tsai Ming-liang's lonely Taipei love triangle won the Golden Lion for good reason.

no-picnic.reason: Another screaming title with zero context.

bridesmaids-15th-anniversary-2026.reason: Wiig and McCarthy are still hilarious but this is basically a Netflix rewatch in a theater.

donkey-days.reason: Either a charming animal doc or very weird art film.

variations-on-a-theme.reason: Sounds like experimental film territory which could work or feel pretentious.

two-mountains-weighing-down-my-chest.reason: That title suggests heavy emotional drama but could be good or overwrought.

momma-s-man.reason: Azazel Jacobs' semi-autobiographical comedy about a man-child returning home is quietly brilliant.

la-maison-des-bois.reason: Classic French arthouse cinema getting a rare theatrical screening deserves your attention. These repertory showings at places like Metrograph don't happen often.

naked-childhood.reason: Maurice Pialat's brutal 1968 debut about a troubled boy bouncing between foster homes is essential French New Wave. A masterclass in unsentimental realism that influenced decades of filmmaking.

to-our-loves.reason: Pialat's raw coming-of-age drama about teenage sexuality and family dysfunction is uncompromising cinema. If it's screening theatrically, it's because it's genuinely important.

advance-screening-hokum-2026.reason: Director Damian McCarthy proved himself with Oddity's genuinely creepy atmosphere. Adam Scott at a haunted inn with mushroom-spiked milk sounds like horror done right.

michael-2026-2026.reason: The MJ biopic focuses on his early solo career and Jackson Five days rather than the controversies. Musical biopics are hit or miss but this one has decent scope.

advance-screening-over-your-dead-body-20.reason: Same as the regular screening - toxic couple murder plot has potential if the execution delivers.

bridesmaids-15th-anniversary-movie-party.reason: The Kristen Wiig comedy holds up as a solid R-rated laughfest with great ensemble work. Anniversary screenings are fun if you're in the mood for group viewing.

ferris-bueller-s-day-off-1986.reason: John Hughes' 1986 classic about the ultimate day of high school rebellion never gets old. Matthew Broderick's charm and Chicago scenery deserve the big screen.

dance-freak-with-live-q-a-2025.reason: Micro-budget sci-fi horror about genetic experiments gone wrong made for $45,000. The sheer DIY audacity plus filmmaker Q&A makes it worth experiencing.

strange-journey-the-story-of-rocky-horro.reason: Documentary about how a London theater play became the ultimate midnight movie cult phenomenon. Rocky Horror devotees and film history buffs will eat this up.

miss-congeniality-movie-party-2000.reason: Sandra Bullock as FBI agent going undercover at beauty pageant is peak early 2000s comedy. Quote-along screenings work if you're feeling nostalgic.

the-big-lebowski-movie-party-1999.reason: The Coen Brothers' stoner noir about The Dude's rug-based adventure through LA's underworld. Cult classics work better in theaters with fellow devotees.

power-to-the-people-john-yoko-live-in-ny.reason: Restored concert film of John Lennon's only full shows after The Beatles at Madison Square Garden. Historic performances with Stevie Wonder and others deserve theatrical treatment.

f-k-my-son-dance-freak-double-feature-20.reason: Exploitation horror comedy double feature based on underground comics about kidnapping and basement horror. Extreme niche appeal for transgressive cinema fans only.

that-time-i-got-reincarnated-as-a-slime-.reason: Anime film about demon lord slime and ogre survivor promises adventure and friendship themes. Series fans will appreciate the big screen treatment, others should skip.

michael-2026-hdr-by-barco-early-access-2.reason: A cradle-to-grave Michael Jackson biopic focusing on his Jackson Five origins and early solo career. For die-hard MJ fans who need the big screen experience.

over-your-dead-body-2026-with-live-q-a-2.reason: A toxic couple tries to murder each other at a remote cabin until dangerous intruders crash the party. Dark comedy premise that could be fun or just nasty depending on the execution.

everyone-is-lying-to-you-for-money-with-.reason: Ben McKenzie investigates the crypto industry's culture of hype and fraud through major scandals and collapses. Smart, accessible documentary about how financial speculation ruins real people's lives, plus you can ask questions.

lee-cronin-s-the-mummy-hdr-by-barco-2026.reason: A journalist's daughter disappears in the desert and returns eight years later as something horrific. Lee Cronin made Evil Dead Rise, so this could be solid horror or generic franchise fodder.

m-rama-with-live-q-a-2026.reason: A young Māori woman travels to Yorkshire to confront the Englishman whose colonial violence destroyed her family. Sounds like a fierce revenge story about historical trauma with a Q&A to dig deeper.

the-school-duel-with-live-q-a-2024.reason: In dystopian Florida, the governor creates a televised fight-to-the-death competition for schoolchildren. Brazenly political horror that tackles gun violence head-on.

the-super-mario-galaxy-movie-hdr-by-barc.reason: Another animated Mario movie from the team behind the billion-dollar 2023 film. Pure franchise content designed to sell Nintendo products to kids.

mystery-transmission-035-1900.reason: Monthly series presenting mystery films from the outer limits of home video history. You never know what weird gem they'll dig up from the underground.

a-beautiful-planet.reason: IMAX nature docs are made for the theater experience - this is what big screens were invented for.

wasteman.reason: Perfect RT score for what's likely UK social realism - trust the critical consensus here.

hunting-matthew-nichols.reason: No plot details or scores make this a complete wild card.

beast.reason: No details provided but 92% RT suggests quality, though could be any number of films titled Beast.
beast.consensus: Limited information with strong critical score.

chao.reason: Don't know this one well enough to judge. Probably experimental or foreign arthouse.

newborn.reason: No plot details available but likely intimate character drama. Hard to recommend without knowing what it's about.

ready-or-not-2-here-i-come.reason: The first film was a tight horror-comedy that didn't need a sequel. This screams cash grab without the original's surprise factor.

a-great-awakening.reason: No plot details available but the title suggests spiritual or religious themes. Without more info, it's a gamble.

reminders-of-him.reason: No plot details available but sounds like romantic drama territory. Can't judge without knowing the story.

forbidden-fruits.reason: 54% RT score with no other data points suggests a forgettable indie that couldn't land.

that-time-i-got-reincarnated-as-a-slime-.reason: If you're deep into the anime series, sure, but it won't convert anyone new.

faces-of-death.reason: The original exploitation doc is a relic that should stay buried.

under-the-sea-3d.reason: Generic underwater nature documentary. The 3D gimmick isn't worth the trip unless you're desperate for aquarium vibes.

dacoit.reason: Title suggests an Indian action film about bandits or outlaws. Could be a solid genre piece if you're into subcontinental cinema.

love-insurance-kompany.reason: Sounds like a romantic comedy involving an insurance company. Probably lightweight entertainment that depends entirely on the leads' chemistry.

busboys.reason: Restaurant industry dramedy following the lives of busboys. Could be a heartfelt working-class story or generic service industry comedy.

bad-boy-karthik.reason: Generic-sounding Indian action film about a rebellious protagonist. Title screams formulaic commercial cinema without much substance.

salmokji-whispering-water.reason: Sounds like a contemplative film about water or environmental themes. Could be a meditative art film or nature documentary.

fireflies-at-el-mozote.reason: Likely a documentary about the El Mozote massacre in El Salvador. Important historical subject matter that deserves attention.

oru-durooha-saahacharyathil.reason: Malayalam film with a title suggesting mystery or adventure. Could be engaging regional cinema if you're open to subtitles.

panda-plan-2.reason: Sequel to what was probably a mediocre action comedy starring Jackie Chan. More of the same tired animal-themed stunts.

golden-boy.reason: Clifford Odets' Depression-era play about a violinist who becomes a boxer to escape poverty. Classic American theater that still hits hard about talent versus survival.

the-true-story-of-tamara-de-lempicka-the.reason: Documentary about the Art Deco painter who fled the Russian Revolution and reinvented herself in Paris. For art history buffs who like stories about resilient women.

tn-2026.reason: Generic sci-fi title that screams low-budget future dystopia. Probably has more ambition than budget to pull it off.

a-little-something-extra.reason: French comedy about a man with Down syndrome pursuing independence and love. Sweet premise but these feel-good dramedies can go either saccharine or genuine.

ph-1.reason: Mysterious title suggests either pretentious experimental film or straight-to-streaming horror. Either way, not worth the theater trip.

cave-of-forgotten-dreams-the-3d-experien.reason: Werner Herzog exploring the Chauvet Cave's 30,000-year-old paintings in 3D. Herzog plus ancient art plus immersive format equals essential viewing.

over-your-dead-body-early-access.reason: Takashi Miike horror film about actors in a ghost story who start living it. Miike delivers weird Japanese horror but this isn't his most accessible work.

i-love-boosters-fashion-forward-filanthr.reason: A crew of professional shoplifters target a cutthroat fashion mogul in Boots Riley's follow-up to Sorry to Bother You, starring Keke Palmer and LaKeith Stanfield. Best for people who want sharp social satire wrapped in heist movie fun.

michael-2026-hdr-by-barco-2026.reason: A biographical drama tracing Michael Jackson's journey from the Jackson Five to global superstar, focusing on his creative ambitions and iconic performances. Best for MJ devotees who want the full theatrical experience with premium sound and picture.

eyes-without-a-face-1960.reason: A mad scientist kidnaps young women to graft their faces onto his disfigured daughter in this 1960 French horror masterpiece that mixes fairy tale poetry with surgical nightmares. Best for horror lovers who appreciate artful filmmaking over cheap scares.

mystery-transmission-036-1900.reason: A monthly curated screening of underground films, surreal docs, and outsider experiments presented as if a cable station was invaded by otherworldly creatures. Best for adventurous viewers who like discovering weird cinema without knowing what they're getting into.

caliber-9-1972.reason: A small-time gangster fresh out of prison tries to convince everyone he's gone straight while they hunt for his hidden stolen money in this stylized 1972 Italian crime thriller. Best for people who want to see where Tarantino got his ideas.

the-unholy-rollers-1972.reason: A factory worker becomes a ruthless roller derby queen who drinks cheap beer, curses like a sailor, and skates to win in this 1972 exploitation classic starring Claudia Jennings. Best for anyone who misses when movies let women be complete assholes.

turtle-vision-1991.reason: A cameraman filming covert adult content becomes obsessed with a woman who slashes her clients with a boxcutter in this 1991 Japanese pink film about voyeurism and urban decay. Best for adventurous cinephiles who can handle disturbing psychosexual material.

legally-blonde-25th-anniversary-2001.reason: A sorority queen follows her ex to Harvard Law School and discovers she's much smarter than anyone expected in this bubbly comedy that launched Reese Witherspoon to stardom. Best for anyone who needs to remember that pink can be powerful.

the-amazing-circus-the-last-act-2026.reason: A feature-length conclusion to the viral internet series combines episode 8 with a new hour-long finale about digital circus performers facing eternity and trauma. Best for people already invested in this specific online animation series.

meek-s-cutoff.reason: A slow-burn western following Oregon Trail pioneers lost in the desert with an unreliable guide. Best for patient viewers who like atmospheric filmmaking and feminist takes on frontier stories.

gorillas-in-the-mist-the-story-of-dian-f.reason: A biographical drama about the primatologist who studied mountain gorillas in Rwanda until her murder. Best for nature lovers and fans of Sigourney Weaver's powerhouse performance.

swapped.reason: An anime about two students who switch bodies and navigate high school drama. Best for completionist anime fans who have already seen every other body-swap story.

the-black-stallion.reason: A boy and wild horse survive a shipwreck and form a deep bond on a desert island before racing glory. Best for anyone who loves pure cinema magic and stories about trust between species.

power-to-the-people-john-yoko-live-in-ny.reason: A documentary about John Lennon and Yoko Ono's political activism during their New York years. Best for Beatles fanatics and those interested in 1970s counterculture history.

billie-eilish-hit-me-hard-and-soft-the-t.reason: A 3D concert film capturing Billie Eilish's latest tour with immersive audio and visuals. Best for devoted fans who want the big screen concert experience without the crowds.

that-time-i-got-reincarnated-as-a-slime-.reason: An anime film about a corporate worker reborn as a slime monster building his own kingdom. Best for fans of the series who need every piece of content regardless of quality.

the-silence-of-the-lambs-35th-anniversar.reason: A FBI trainee consults imprisoned cannibal Hannibal Lecter to catch another serial killer. Best for horror fans and anyone who wants to see a perfect thriller on the big screen.

eraserheads-combo-on-the-run.reason: A documentary about the Filipino rock band that defined 1990s Philippine alternative music. Best for fans of Philippine rock history and Southeast Asian music culture.

one-spoon-of-chocolate.reason: A feel-good drama about family recipes and healing through cooking. Best for viewers who find comfort in predictable food-centric stories.

the-story-of-everything.reason: A documentary attempting to explain the universe from Big Bang to present day in feature length. Best for viewers who prefer surface-level science over actual depth.

jetlee.reason: An action film about martial arts and revenge with unknown production quality. Best for viewers desperate for any martial arts content regardless of execution.

one-ok-rock-detox-japan-tour-2025-at-nis.reason: A concert film of the Japanese rock band's stadium performance with full production value. Best for fans of the band who want the arena experience in theaters.

raja-shivaji.reason: A historical drama about the 17th century Maratha warrior king who fought Mughal expansion. Best for fans of Indian historical epics and Shivaji devotees.

ek-din.reason: A Bengali drama about family relationships and daily struggles in contemporary life. Best for viewers specifically seeking regional Indian cinema content.

cabin-fever-director-s-cut-relive-the-ho.reason: An extended version of Eli Roth's flesh-eating virus horror film with additional gore and scenes. Best for horror completists who need every version of mediocre movies.

pitt-siyapa.reason: A Punjabi comedy about misunderstandings and family chaos with predictable setups. Best for viewers who enjoy regional humor regardless of script quality.

f1.reason: A racing drama following Formula 1 drivers through the pressures and dangers of professional motorsport. Best for racing fans who want big screen speed and Brad Pitt star power.

tt31514146.reason: A biographical drama about John Davidson's struggle with Tourette's syndrome and the social isolation it caused. Best for viewers interested in disability narratives and personal triumph stories.

strange-journey-the-story-of-rocky-horro.reason: A documentary tracing how a small London theater production became the ultimate midnight movie phenomenon. Best for anyone who's ever thrown rice at a screen or wondered how cult classics are born.

the-rider.reason: A drama about a rodeo cowboy recovering from a head injury, starring real cowboys including the actual person the story is based on. Best for anyone who wants authentic American storytelling without Hollywood polish.

tt37016430.reason: A documentary following Northern Ireland loyalists attempting to build the world's tallest bonfire on their estate. Best for viewers drawn to community stories and cultural traditions they've never seen before.

tt0060107.reason: A three-hour Tarkovsky epic about a 15th-century Russian icon painter navigating faith and artistic creation amid political chaos. Best for serious cinephiles ready to surrender to slow cinema and spiritual questioning.

obsession-2026-2026.reason: A romantic horror about a guy who breaks a magic willow tree to win over his crush and faces dark consequences. Best for nobody unless you're desperate for supernatural rom-com schlock.

eo.reason: A Polish film that follows a donkey's journey through modern Europe, told from the animal's perspective. Best for art house fans who don't mind experimental narratives and want something genuinely unique.

the-iron-giant.reason: A 1999 animated film about a boy who befriends a giant robot during the Cold War. Best for anyone who wants to see one of the best animated films ever made on a big screen.

tt0910970.reason: A lonely robot cleaning up post-apocalyptic Earth falls in love and accidentally saves humanity. Best for date nights and anyone who needs to remember why Pixar used to be untouchable.

tt0073076.reason: A documentary about Jackie Kennedy's eccentric relatives living in squalor in their decaying East Hampton mansion. Best for those fascinated by American decay and the thin line between charm and madness.

tt13874422.reason: A mother and daughter return to a haunted hotel that holds family secrets in this atmospheric chamber piece. Best for Joanna Hogg devotees and viewers who prefer psychological tension over jump scares.

tt2140203.reason: A single mother raises two half-werewolf children while keeping their supernatural nature secret from the world. Best for anime newcomers and parents who understand that raising kids is already basically dealing with wild animals.

tt37846982.reason: A documentary exploring land access rights and power structures that keep people from nature. Best for outdoorsy types and viewers interested in environmental justice issues.

whisper-of-the-heart.reason: A Studio Ghibli film about a teenage girl who discovers her passion for writing. Best for people who want gentle coming-of-age magic without the fantasy elements of other Ghibli films.

past-lives.reason: A drama about childhood friends from Korea who reconnect as adults in New York decades later. Best for anyone who wants to feel things deeply and thinks about paths not taken.

two-seasons-two-strangers.reason: An independent film that appears to be about encounters between strangers across different time periods. Best for viewers who like quiet character studies and non-linear storytelling.

tt12042730.reason: A science teacher wakes up alone on a spaceship with amnesia, gradually remembering his mission to save Earth from solar extinction. Best for sci-fi lovers who want smart problem-solving and unexpected friendship in space.

infernal-affairs.reason: A Hong Kong thriller about an undercover cop infiltrating the mob while a mob informant infiltrates the police. Best for people who want to see the brilliant original that Scorsese remade as The Departed.

hero.reason: A Zhang Yimou martial arts epic about assassins plotting to kill China's first emperor, told in multiple color-coded versions. Best for people who want their action movies to look like moving paintings.

i-love-boosters-2026.reason: A crew of professional shoplifters targets a ruthless fashion executive in this heist comedy from the director of Sorry to Bother You. Best for viewers who enjoyed Boots Riley's previous anti-capitalist chaos and want more Keke Palmer being brilliant.

star-wars-episode-iv-a-new-hope.reason: A 1977 space opera about a farm boy who joins a rebellion against an evil empire. Best for experiencing the most influential blockbuster ever made on a big screen where it belongs.

alien.reason: A dark sequel where Ripley crashes on a prison planet full of religious fanatics and faces another xenomorph. Best for Alien completists and people who like their sci-fi horror nihilistic and weird.

no-country-for-old-men.reason: A Coen Brothers thriller about a drug deal gone wrong and the psychopathic killer hunting everyone involved. Best for people who like their Westerns existential and their villains unforgettable.

polyester.reason: A John Waters comedy about a suburban housewife whose life falls apart, originally released with scratch-and-sniff Odorama cards. Best for people who like their satire trashy and their filmmakers genuinely subversive.

tt36589928.reason: A comedy-drama-thriller about young American men navigating modern life and expectations. Best for viewers interested in millennial male anxiety and genre-blending independent cinema.

flowers-of-shanghai.reason: A Hou Hsiao-hsien film set entirely in 1880s Shanghai brothels, shot in long static takes with natural lighting. Best for patient viewers who want cinema as meditation and atmosphere over plot.

sorry-to-bother-you-2018.reason: A telemarketer discovers his "white voice" leads to success but also a bizarre corporate conspiracy involving horse-people. Best for viewers who want their social satire served with complete insanity and aren't afraid of movies that go completely off the rails.

everything-everywhere-all-at-once.reason: A multiverse action comedy about a Chinese-American laundromat owner who must save reality from her daughter. Best for people who want their existential crises served with googly eyes and hot dog fingers.

tt0071115.reason: A newly widowed woman hits the road with her son, determined to restart her singing career. Best for viewers who want to see Scorsese direct a character study about female resilience instead of mob violence.

hard-boiled.reason: A John Woo action film about a cop and an undercover agent teaming up to take down arms dealers in Hong Kong. Best for people who want to see the pinnacle of stylized gun violence choreography.

alo-se-preceded-by-qui-donc-a-r-v.reason: A Swiss film about outsider artist Aloïse Corbaz preceded by a short. For fans of European arthouse cinema about troubled artists.

basquiat-black-and-white-version.reason: A documentary about Jean-Michel Basquiat presented in black and white. For art fans who want to understand one of the most important painters of the 80s.

carrie-mae-weems-selects-no-country-for-.reason: A Coen Brothers masterpiece chosen by photographer Carrie Mae Weems as part of an artist selection series. For anyone who appreciates when great artists recommend great films.

forever-a-woman.reason: A drama about gender identity and personal transformation. For viewers interested in LGBTQ+ stories and character-driven narratives.

hiroshi-sugimoto-selects-the-face-of-ano.reason: A 1966 Teshigahara psychological thriller about identity chosen by renowned photographer Hiroshi Sugimoto. For fans of Japanese New Wave and conceptual photography crossover.

i-have-graduated.reason: A coming-of-age story about post-graduation uncertainty. For recent graduates or anyone feeling lost about their next life chapter.

lee-friedlander-selects-north-by-northwe.reason: A Hitchcock classic chosen by legendary street photographer Lee Friedlander. For anyone who wants to see a master thriller through the eyes of a visual artist.

marie-and-bruce.reason: A stage adaptation about a toxic marriage starring Matthew Broderick and Parker Posey. For masochists who enjoy watching people be awful to each other for 90 minutes.

martine-gutierrez-selects-princess-monon.reason: A Miyazaki environmental epic in its original Japanese chosen by interdisciplinary artist Martine Gutierrez. For Studio Ghibli purists and anyone who wants the film as the director intended.

muriel-or-the-time-of-return.reason: A 1963 Alain Resnais film about memory and the Algerian War starring Delphine Seyrig. For French New Wave enthusiasts and viewers who like complex narrative structures.

naked-came-the-stranger.reason: A 1970s adaptation of a deliberately trashy novel written as a publishing experiment. For people with very specific tastes in exploitation cinema history.

nan-goldin-selects-the-naked-kiss.reason: A 1964 Sam Fuller crime film about a reformed prostitute chosen by photographer Nan Goldin. For fans of pulp noir and seeing how contemporary artists view classic American cinema.

speciwomen-focus-kim-torres.reason: A showcase of experimental filmmaker Kim Torres' work. For fans of underground cinema and feminist experimental film.

the-designated-mourner.reason: A Wallace Shawn play adaptation about intellectuals during political upheaval. For theater lovers and people who enjoy dense philosophical conversations.

wardell-milan-selects-hereditary.reason: A modern horror masterpiece about family trauma chosen by visual artist Wardell Milan. For horror fans and anyone who wants to see the genre taken seriously as art.

where-is-the-friend-s-house.reason: A 1987 Abbas Kiarostami film about a boy trying to return his friend's notebook. For anyone who wants to see Iranian cinema at its most humanistic and beautiful.

tt14622972.reason: A documentary exploring Leonard Bernstein's complex personality and musical genius beyond his public image. Best for classical music lovers and those interested in American cultural history.

mad-bills-to-pay.reason: An independent film about financial stress and working-class struggles. For viewers interested in low-budget American independent cinema about real economic anxiety.

tt0091638.reason: A black-and-white time capsule of 1985 East Village featuring musicians, poets, and street life before gentrification. Best for New York nostalgia seekers and those who love authentic urban documentation.

tt0098197.reason: A drama about friendship across religious lines in 1930s Germany as Nazi power rises. Best for those interested in personal stories set against historical turning points.

bellissima.reason: A 1951 Luchino Visconti film about a mother pushing her daughter into show business. For Italian cinema fans and anyone who wants to see Anna Magnani at her most powerful.

teenage-mutant-ninja-turtles.reason: A franchise reboot about pizza-loving reptile warriors fighting crime in New York. For nostalgic parents and kids who have never seen good action movies.

original-sound.reason: A documentary about music production and sound design in film. For audio enthusiasts and film students interested in the technical side of moviemaking.

the-shining.reason: A Kubrick horror masterpiece about isolation and madness in a haunted hotel. For anyone who wants to see one of the greatest horror films ever made on the big screen.

invisible-borders.reason: A documentary about migration and border crossing in contemporary Africa. For viewers interested in current global politics and African perspectives on migration.

happy-together.reason: A Wong Kar-wai masterpiece about a toxic gay relationship in Buenos Aires starring Tony Leung and Leslie Cheung. For anyone who wants gorgeous cinematography and devastating emotional storytelling.

bullet-in-the-head.reason: A John Woo action epic about three friends who flee Hong Kong for Vietnam during the war and get caught in chaos. Best for fans of operatic gunplay and brotherhood sagas who want peak Hong Kong cinema.

stage-russia-eugene-onegin.reason: A filmed stage production of Pushkin's classic opera about unrequited love and regret in 19th century Russia. Best for opera lovers who prefer theater recordings to live performances.

adaptation-2002.reason: A Charlie Kaufman meta-comedy about a screenwriter's struggle to adapt an unadaptable book that becomes brilliantly self-aware. Best for anyone who likes smart, weird movies that break the fourth wall.

the-handmaiden-book-club-screening-2016.reason: A Park Chan-wook erotic thriller about deception and desire between two women in Japanese-occupied Korea. Best for those who like stylish, twisted narratives with beautiful cinematography and aren't squeamish about sexuality.

prismatic-ground-closing-night-gangsteri.reason: A curated program of experimental films exploring crime themes through an avant-garde lens. Best for adventurous viewers who like their cinema challenging and abstract.

the-hour-of-liberation-has-arrived.reason: A documentary about political liberation movements that I'm not familiar with the specifics of. Best for viewers interested in political history and social justice documentaries.

dhurandhar-the-revenge.reason: A Bollywood action film that sounds like direct-to-streaming revenge fare. Best for people who will watch literally any action movie regardless of quality.

private-event-today-in-theater-commissar.reason: A private event that you literally cannot attend. Best for people who enjoy being locked out of things.

anatomy-of-a-murder.reason: A 1959 courtroom drama from Otto Preminger starring James Stewart as a lawyer defending a murder case. Best for anyone who likes smart legal thrillers and classic Hollywood at its peak.

kes.reason: A 1969 British film about a working-class boy who trains a kestrel hawk as his escape from a grim life. Best for people who want their coming-of-age stories raw and unsentimental.

blue-heron.reason: An independent film that I'm not familiar with the specifics of. Best for viewers who like discovering new indie filmmakers and character-driven stories.

deep-water.reason: A psychological thriller starring Ben Affleck and Ana de Armas as a married couple playing dangerous games. Best for people who miss boring erotic thrillers from the 90s but with worse acting.

la-la-land.reason: A musical about an aspiring actress and jazz musician falling in love in Los Angeles. Best for people who want their musicals pretty and melancholy rather than energetic and fun.

deliverance.reason: A 1972 thriller about four city men whose canoe trip in rural Georgia turns into a nightmare of violence and survival. Best for people who want their masculinity examined through genuine terror.

vanya-on-42nd-street.reason: A Louis Malle film that documents a theater company rehearsing Chekhov's Uncle Vanya in a crumbling Broadway theater. Best for theater lovers and anyone who wants to see great acting in its purest form.

chungking-express.reason: A Wong Kar-wai film about two Hong Kong cops dealing with love and loneliness in their own separate stories. Best for people who like their romance stylish, melancholy, and told through pop music and neon lights.

red-cliff.reason: A John Woo epic about the famous Three Kingdoms battle in ancient China, featuring massive naval warfare and political intrigue. Best for people who want their historical epics grand in scale and beautiful to look at.

the-smiling-lieutenant.reason: A Lubitsch musical about a Viennese officer caught between two women. Best for anyone who wants to see why they called it the Lubitsch touch.

harry-and-tonto.reason: An elderly widower drives cross-country with his cat after being evicted from his apartment. Best for viewers who connect with quiet character studies about aging and displacement.

hill-of-freedom.reason: A Japanese man searches for his ex-girlfriend in Seoul through fragmented narrative episodes. Best for Hong Sang-soo devotees who enjoy his specific brand of rambling relationship comedy.

wendy-and-lucy.reason: A woman and her dog get stranded in Oregon while driving to Alaska for work. Best for viewers who appreciate minimal storytelling about economic precarity.

clueless.reason: A Beverly Hills teen plays matchmaker while navigating high school social politics. Best for anyone who wants smart comedy that actually understands teenage life.

cyclo.reason: A young cyclo driver in Saigon gets pulled into the criminal underworld to survive. Best for viewers ready for Tran Anh Hung's intense urban poetry.

the-emperor-and-the-assassin.reason: An epic about China's first emperor and the woman who tries to kill him. Best for fans of sweeping historical dramas who have three hours to spare.

eagles-of-the-republic.reason: A documentary about the making of a Chinese propaganda film. Best for viewers interested in the intersection of cinema and political messaging.

hahaha.reason: A comedy about two men who discover they vacationed in the same seaside town and loved the same woman. Best for Hong Sang-soo fans who enjoy his circular relationship comedies.

painters-painting.reason: A documentary about the New York art scene and Abstract Expressionist movement from the 1940s-70s. Best for anyone interested in how American art found its voice after World War II.

9-to-5.reason: A comedy about three office workers who kidnap their sexist boss and run the company themselves. Best for workplace comedy fans who want actual teeth with their laughs.

animal-farm.reason: An animated adaptation of Orwell's allegory about farm animals overthrowing their human owner. Best for viewers who want political satire in animated form.

the-river-wild.reason: A family rafting trip turns deadly when they encounter armed fugitives on the water. Best for fans of 90s thriller formulas with outdoor adventure settings.

ricky.reason: A French film about a baby who grows wings and can fly. Best for viewers who want surreal family drama that doesn't quite work as either.

over-your-dead-body.reason: A Japanese horror film about actors rehearsing a ghost story who begin living out the supernatural tale. Best for Takashi Miike fans prepared for his blend of theater and horror.

peter-pan.reason: A live-action adaptation of the classic story about the boy who never grows up. Best for families wanting a traditional telling without Disney's animation.

the-mountain.reason: A period drama about a young man traveling with a doctor who performs lobotomies. Best for viewers drawn to cold, clinical examinations of medical history.

southland-tales.reason: A dystopian satire about celebrity culture and politics starring The Rock and Sarah Michelle Gellar. Best for viewers who think Richard Kelly's ambitious mess deserves three hours of their time.

fallen-angels.reason: A neon-soaked crime film about hitmen, mute ex-cons, and late-night encounters in Hong Kong. Best for Wong Kar-wai fans who want his style applied to pulp fiction.

desert-warrior.reason: An action film about tribal warfare in ancient Arabia. Best for viewers with very low expectations for historical action movies.

apex.reason: A generic action thriller about survival games or corporate conspiracy. For people who think Die Hard needed less personality and more CGI.

boulevard.reason: A drama about a married man questioning his sexuality late in life. For viewers who want intimate character studies over plot-driven entertainment.

touki-bouki.reason: A 1973 Senegalese masterpiece about young lovers dreaming of escape to Paris. For anyone interested in African cinema or visually stunning art films that broke new ground.

bill-plympton-grilled-by-signe-baumane.reason: A documentary where animator Signe Baumane interviews cult animation legend Bill Plympton about his career. For animation fans and anyone curious about underground art-making.

nausica-of-the-valley-of-the-wind.reason: A Miyazaki epic about a princess fighting to save her world from environmental collapse and giant insects. For Studio Ghibli fans and anyone who wants spectacular hand-drawn animation with real themes.

the-mystery-of-picasso-rembrandt-painter.reason: A double bill of art documentaries showing master painters at work. For art lovers who want to see creative genius captured on film.

a-bigger-splash-mark-turbyfill-by-markop.reason: A David Hockney documentary paired with experimental filmmaker Gregory Markopoulos. For art house devotees who appreciate avant-garde cinema and visual artists.
